pattern: Walking the list once and counting a component only at the position where a chosen value is followed by a non-chosen value (or the end) tallies the maximal runs without explicit grouping.
hints: A component is a maximal run of list nodes whose values are all in the chosen set, so you only need to detect where one run ends.
Put the chosen values in a hash set, then scan the list and notice the boundaries: a run finishes when the current node is chosen but the next node is absent or not chosen.
Count one each time a node's value is in the set and either it is the last node or the following node's value is not in the set.

edge_inputs.description: first example, a gap splits the chosen values into two runs
second example, two separated pairs form two runs
single node that is chosen, one component
all nodes chosen and contiguous, exactly one component
every chosen node isolated by an unchosen neighbour
chosen values only at the two ends
run that ends exactly at the tail

stress.description: permutation list of 0..n-1 with a random subset chosen
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 9999))
    order = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(order)
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    chosen = rng.sample(range(n), k)
    return [order, chosen]
stress.note: values are unique and within 0..n-1 per the constraints
